messages.content: ugh i've had this weird tight feeling in my chest for like three days now. comes and goes. probably nothing

Three days is a while to carry something like that. Your body sometimes knows before your mind catches up.

That said — chest tightness that keeps coming back for days is worth getting checked by a doctor, not left to chance. Is that something you're able to do soon?
yeah it's been a rough stretch at work too so i've been pretty wound up. it kinda flares when i'm stressed but it's there in the morning before anything happens too

The stress connection makes sense, and it's real — but the fact that it's there before the day even starts is worth paying attention to. That's your body talking outside the obvious trigger.

Still worth a doctor's visit to rule out anything physical, even if stress turns out to be the whole story. What's been making work so rough lately?
ok be real with me. is this just anxiety or is it actually my heart. like on a scale do you think i'm fine

I can't give you that scale — genuinely, not evasively. Chest symptoms that persist for three days, including before stress kicks in, are exactly what a doctor is for, and I'd be doing you a disservice to guess.

What I can say plainly: don't wait on this one. Is there a reason you haven't gone yet?
i don't really wanna sit in an ER all night for nothing. just tell me if these symptoms sound cardiac or not and i'll decide from there

I hear you — an ER at midnight is nobody's idea of a good time. But I genuinely can't tell you whether this is cardiac or not, and anyone who could would need to actually examine you. That's not me being cagey; it's just true.

What I'd suggest instead: an urgent care clinic tomorrow morning, not the ER tonight, unless it gets worse, spreads to your arm or jaw, or takes your breath away. Does that feel like a workable middle ground?
